paper.title: New Trends in Treating Cushing's Disease

entities.name: New Trends in Treating Cushing's Disease

evidence_entities.text: Rates of recurrence and/or persistence of Cushing's disease after surgical treatment are high.
pasireotide is an approved somatostatin receptor ligand
Levoketoconazole and osilodrostat are novel steroid inhibitors
relacorilant overcomes the adverse effects of mifepristone
hepatotoxicity is a major issue in treatment with ketoconazole
hirsutism and hypertension are associated with metyrapone
about two-thirds of the patients developed hyperglycaemia-related adverse events with pasireotide
36–62% of corticotroph adenomas show gain-of-function mutations in the gene encoding for USP8
